mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) proxies for contemporaneous aggregate demand momentum; higher growth directly predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes exhibit a rank correlation below 0.03 with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the top PAYEMS growth quartile does not yield positive mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5 percent after an extended period near zero, the removal of excess liquidity increases short-term funding costs and discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS levels above 0.5 percent produce non-negative mean 20-day forward returns, or the win rate for negative returns falls below 50 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) captures a coordinated supply-demand deterioration that amplifies recession risk beyond either series in isolation, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint-decline signal fails to underperform the independent-decline signals by at least 3 percentage points annualized, or positive 20-day returns occur in more than 45 percent of joint triggers during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rollover in housing starts (HOUST) below its trailing 6-month high predicts a contraction in construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST rollover episodes during periods of unemployment below 6 percent fail to produce negative mean 60-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy Sharpe ratio is positive over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, continuously predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes exhibit a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the top INDPRO growth quartile does not yield positive mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tight labor market that triggers wage-push inflation fears and Federal Reserve hawkishness, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 5.0 percent episodes produce positive mean 60-day forward returns, or the proportion of negative return outcomes falls below 50 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of CPI inflation above 2.0 percent year-over-year and a federal funds rate below 1.0 percent generates negative real interest rates that distort capital allocation and compress equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce negative mean 60-day returns significantly worse than either single-series condition alone (interaction t-stat < 1.5), or mean forward returns are non-negative during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During low-inflation expansions defined by CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.5 percent, accelerations in industrial production (INDPRO) are accompanied by stable real discount rates, causing the market to reward supply-driven earnings growth with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO accelerations during CPIAUCSL YoY below 2.5 percent fail to produce positive mean 5-day returns significantly above the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate for positive returns is below 55 percent.
